task_completion.description: Agent navigates to the clinical trial analysis workspace.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal or file explorer showing the directory /home/ga/workspace/clinical_trial_analysis/.

task_completion.description: Agent opens the Python pipeline files for auditing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A code editor or IDE (like VS Code, Vim, or Nano) displaying the Python scripts from the clinical trial pipeline.

task_completion.description: Agent identifies and fixes statistical methodology errors in the code.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code editor showing active modifications to statistical calculations, such as p-values, confidence intervals, sample size logic, or statistical tests.

task_completion.description: Agent identifies and fixes regulatory compliance errors in the code.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code editor showing active modifications to regulatory logic, such as data blinding, handling of missing data, or FDA reporting standards.

task_completion.description: Agent runs the fixed pipeline to ensure it meets standards.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal window showing the execution of the Python pipeline script with successful completion output and no error tracebacks.

integrity.description: Agent uses a real text editor or IDE to modify the files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of a standard editor interface (e.g., VS Code, Vim) rather than just echoing text into files blindly from the command line.

integrity.description: Agent fixes the actual logic rather than bypassing checks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Code changes should show corrected mathematical or regulatory logic, not just commenting out assertions, deleting validation steps, or hardcoding a 'pass' message.

integrity.description: Pipeline execution occurs in a genuine shell environment.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal prompt and standard output from the Python interpreter, rather than a text file edited to look like terminal output.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task involves a Python pipeline at /home/ga/workspace/clinical_trial_analysis/ processing synthetic clinical trial data for 200 patients. There are exactly 6 seeded statistical and regulatory methodology bugs in the code. The agent must fix enough bugs to reach a 60-point threshold (each fixed bug is worth 16-17 points).
